key_insight: A diffusion-based transformer with adaptive routing can better capture subtle peritumoral MRI patterns for predicting perineural invasion (PNI) in cholangiocarcinoma while reducing unnecessary computation.
clinical_relevance: Improved preoperative PNI prediction could help refine risk stratification and surgical planning for cholangiocarcinoma patients.
method: Formulated PNI prediction as a diffusion-based classification problem.
Used a transformer-based denoising network to model global MRI context and subtle spatial cues.
Added adaptive routing over attention heads, spatial tokens, and MLP width to improve efficiency.
evidence_signal: In the abstract, the proposed method achieved an AUC of 0.731 with 257.57 GFLOPs on MRI-based PNI prediction.
watch_item: The approach may be computationally efficient relative to full transformer-diffusion models, but the abstract does not provide external validation or details on dataset size and generalizability.
paper_title: Adaptive Routing for Efficient Diffusion Transformer-Based PNI Prediction
